task_completion.description: Open FreeCAD and create a new document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD application window is open with a new active document workspace.

task_completion.description: Create a 2D sketch of the connecting rod base profile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D sketch is visible in the main view showing two circular ends connected by a shank. Dimensions such as 100mm (length), 40mm/20mm (outer diameters), or 30mm/12mm (inner diameters) may be visible in the sketcher.

task_completion.description: Extrude the base sketch into a 3D solid model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D solid model of the connecting rod is visible in the 3D viewport. The Model tree view should show a 'Pad' or extrusion feature.

task_completion.description: Create rectangular pockets on the top and bottom faces of the shank to form an I-beam profile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D model shows recessed rectangular areas (pockets) along the central shank. The Model tree view should ideally show 'Pocket' or similar subtractive features.

task_completion.description: Save the model as connecting_rod.FCStd in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD window title bar, tab name, or the root node in the Model tree view displays 'connecting_rod'. A save dialog or terminal confirmation might show the path '/home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the FreeCAD GUI to build or verify the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the FreeCAD interface with toolbars, 3D viewport, and Model tree, rather than just a terminal window executing a script.

integrity.description: The model was built from scratch using parametric features.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Model tree view (usually on the left) shows a history of operations (e.g., Sketch, Pad, Pocket) rather than a single imported solid or mesh, indicating the agent actually modeled it.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The final model must be saved as 'connecting_rod.FCStd'. The bounding box length should be roughly 115-125 mm (100mm center-to-center + radii) and width 38-42 mm. The total thickness is exactly 10 mm. The big end inner hole radius is 15.0 mm (30 mm diameter) and the small end inner hole radius is 6.0 mm (12 mm diameter). The I-beam pockets are 2 mm deep on both sides, leaving a central web thickness of exactly 6.0 mm.
